整套首页
unit_15 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_15

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 486 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.Biologists have little (i)__________ drawing the link between the success of humanity and human (ii)__________. Indeed, many biologists claim that this attribute, the ability to (iii)__________, or, to put it more sharply, to make individuals subordinate their self-interest to the needs of the group, lies at the root of human achievement.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　compunction about; sociability; cooperate
consensusdrawlieattribute
consensus: standard unit headword | draw: standard unit headword | lie: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
The journey to (i)__________, when it starts from a vantage as (ii)__________ as Dunsany's, is often as (iii)__________ as the path to glory. How did a writer of such talent and renown wind up nearly forgotten?The journey to (i)__________, when it starts from a vantage as (ii)__________ as Dunsany's, is often as (iii)__________ as the path to glory. How did a writer of such talent and renown wind up nearly forgotten?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　obscurity; eminent; unfathomable
eminentreactionarymundanecyclical
eminent: standard unit headword | reactionary: standard unit headword | mundane: standard unit headword | cyclical: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
modelinitiallycircumvent
model: standard unit headword | initially: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?Some ethicists worry that a deeper understanding of the brain may be tantamount to __________: if we discover that free will is an illusion of neural circuitry, how will we hold people responsible for their actions?
答案：E　exculpation
illusiontantamount
illusion: standard unit headword | tantamount: standard unit headword
#5 SE
The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright __________.The company is so old-fashioned and opposed to innovation that it can seem downright __________.
答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
fashionold-fashioned
fashion: standard unit headword | old-fashioned: standard unit headword
#6 TC TRIPLE
It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.It is hardly surprising that technological fixes appear to be, more than ever, the best solution to minimize future rise of atmospheric temperatures. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. That remains so even after excluding certain utopian infatuations with those renewable conversions whose limited, exceedingly diffuse, or hard-to-capture resources (be they jet stream winds or ocean waves) (iii) __________ their becoming energy sources during the next few decades.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude
markedminimizepreclude
marked: standard unit headword | minimize: standard unit headword | preclude: standard unit headword
#7 TC DOUBLE
As the pace of the trial (i)__________, the wait at the beginning of the day became less interminable, and the attorneys’ requests to suspend proceedings for private conferences with the judge, almost invariably granted early on, were routinely (ii)__________.As the pace of the trial (i)__________, the wait at the beginning of the day became less interminable, and the attorneys’ requests to suspend proceedings for private conferences with the judge, almost invariably granted early on, were routinely (ii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　accelerated; rebuffed
interminablegrant
interminable: standard unit headword | grant: standard unit headword

Equal parts primer, chronicle, textbook, and source guide, Henderson's new work should be __________ both laypeople and experts.Equal parts primer, chronicle, textbook, and source guide, Henderson's new work should be __________ both laypeople and experts.
答案：C, F　indispensable to; essential for
chroniclelaypeople
chronicle: standard unit headword | laypeople: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　temptation; occlude
temptationocclude
temptation: standard unit headword | occlude: standard unit headword
#10 SE
The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is __________ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.The environmental threat posed by the New Zealand mud snail, an invasive species, is __________ by the snail’s small size, which facilitates its spread across watersheds through human activity.
答案：C, F　exacerbated; aggravated
extenuateinitiate
extenuate: standard unit headword | initiate: standard unit headword

Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii) __________, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　uncritical joy; unfounded

Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to

Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)__________ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)__________ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)__________. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

Blank 2
答案：B, F　depressing; diverting

Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i) __________: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii) __________ standards.Reviews written by music critic and composer Stephenson were hardly (i) __________: musicians who performed his music could count on sympathetic coverage, while those who ignored him were held to (ii) __________ standards.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disinterested; exacting
minimallucid

#16 TC TRIPLE
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)__________. The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)__________ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)__________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent
fashionindignantupend
fashion: standard unit headword | indignant: standard unit headword | upend: standard unit headword
#17 TC TRIPLE
Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation
dilatorymarker
dilatory: standard unit headword | marker: standard unit headword

Unlike the elected branches of the United Sates government where making personal connections with citizens is (i)__________ and almost (ii)__________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions and even through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.Unlike the elected branches of the United Sates government where making personal connections with citizens is (i)__________ and almost (ii)__________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions and even through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for

#19 TC TRIPLE
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)__________. Instead of (ii)__________ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)__________. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)__________. Instead of (ii)__________ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)__________. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
gleandisorient
glean: standard unit headword | disorient: standard unit headword

To make his defense effectively he will certainly have to give specific examples of the media coverage he finds so (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　lowly calumnies; objectionable

#21 TC SINGLE
Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.Given the many thematic strands that the book seeks to draw together into a continuous cord of narrative, it is perforce a work of __________, which is not to say that the author’s research fails to provide sufficient detail about each of his chosen themes.
答案：A　synthesis
drawperforce
draw: standard unit headword | perforce: standard unit headword

The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.The book's notoriety at the time it was published definitely had as a contributory factor its author's (i) __________, which meant that contemporary readers and critics were (ii) __________ the author's intellectual pedigree and motives. Hence it was also difficult, if not impossible, for the critics to (iii) __________ the book's arguments by engaging in ad hominem attacks. Readers repeatedly sought to discover the book's true provenance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　anonymity; unable to establish; dismiss
provenancedismiss
provenance: standard unit headword | dismiss: standard unit headword
#23 TC SINGLE
Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.Whereas water on Mars could plausibly have been delivered by repeated comet impacts in the geologically recent past, the ice on smaller bodies is more difficult to attribute to __________ source.
答案：A　an exogenous
marattribute
mar: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword

Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.Aerial views of the gigantic stone horse attributed to the Native American Quechan people fail to __________ the considerable artistry required to create the piece: the horse appeared crudely constructed unless carefully examined from the ground.
答案：A, B　manifest; reveal
artistryattribute
artistry: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword

答案：B　stalwart
recast
recast: standard unit headword
#26 SE
One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer __________ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.
答案：D, F　shrieking; caterwauling

答案：B　sycophantic
fashionable

答案：A, F　pacific; dovish

答案：B　obscure

答案：B, E　shackle; stifle

答案：A　verisimilitude

答案：C, E　spirited; vigorous

Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii)__________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poison called batrachotoxins. But the (i)__________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii)__________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Blank 2
Blank 3

Blank 2

Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.Filler claims that after the social welfare programs of the 1960s, belief that the government has an obligation to provide decent housing for citizens who cannot afford it was (i)__________ in the United States by the notion that providing suitable shelter for everyone should be (ii)__________. Thus today in the (iii)__________ of taxpayer-sponsored initiatives, we have volunteer home-construction programs, honorable in intent but pitifully limited in scope.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　supplanted; an act of private charity; absence
initiative
initiative: standard unit headword
#51 SE
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
nomenclature
nomenclature: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　scant; limited

scant: standard unit headword
#53 TC SINGLE
Carmen's affection to her sister, though not __________, is plainly too great to permit a painless departure.Carmen's affection to her sister, though not __________, is plainly too great to permit a painless departure.
答案：B　unbounded
ambivalent
ambivalent: standard unit headword
#54 TC DOUBLE
Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i)__________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudosciences, much of the writing has a (ii)__________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earned scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking
pseudoscience
pseudoscience: standard unit headword
#55 TC TRIPLE
For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated
cynical
cynical: standard unit headword
#56 TC DOUBLE
The novel’s heroine show a remarkable (i)__________ to worship at the altar of youth: in her world, youth is (ii)__________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.The novel’s heroine show a remarkable (i)__________ to worship at the altar of youth: in her world, youth is (ii)__________, while age, by contrast, confers competence and wisdom.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow
disinclination
disinclination: standard unit headword
#57 TC SINGLE
Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would __________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would __________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter
congregate
congregate: standard unit headword
#58 SE
In a number of instances, investors hoping to tap into the region’s meteoric growth have instead faced problems ranging from unpredictable management practices to outright __________.In a number of instances, investors hoping to tap into the region’s meteoric growth have instead faced problems ranging from unpredictable management practices to outright __________.
答案：A, C　malfeasance; fraudulence
hysteria
hysteria: standard unit headword
#59 TC TRIPLE
This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author's choice of format, which is the (i)__________ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)__________ reception that academics - motivated partly by (iii)__________ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards - generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author's choice of format, which is the (i)__________ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)__________ reception that academics - motivated partly by (iii)__________ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards - generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　inverse; scornful; snobbish elitism
inverse
inverse: standard unit headword
#60 SE
The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.The town’s air was consistently __________: depending on the breeze, one might be greeted with the sour effluvia of twenty breweries, choking fumes from the coal factory, or brackish smells from the nearby river.
答案：A, E　malodorous; noisome

#61 TC DOUBLE
The geographer held a (i) __________ view of the succession of theoretical trends (environmental determinism, spatial determinism, and various types of critical theory) in her field, maintaining that theory can (ii) __________ what is transpiring in a complex environment by focusing excessively on the favored schemes and variables of the moment.The geographer held a (i) __________ view of the succession of theoretical trends (environmental determinism, spatial determinism, and various types of critical theory) in her field, maintaining that theory can (ii) __________ what is transpiring in a complex environment by focusing excessively on the favored schemes and variables of the moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　deprecatory; obfuscate
magnify
magnify: standard unit headword
#62 TC TRIPLE
Some scholars have interpreted marks incised on pottery during the Neolithic period, some nearly 6,000 years old, as the (i) __________ of writing. But while the marks do appear to have been meaningful symbols, and while the use of symbols is (ii) __________ writing, there is (iii) __________ that the pottery symbols evolved into a true script.Some scholars have interpreted marks incised on pottery during the Neolithic period, some nearly 6,000 years old, as the (i) __________ of writing. But while the marks do appear to have been meaningful symbols, and while the use of symbols is (ii) __________ writing, there is (iii) __________ that the pottery symbols evolved into a true script.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　forerunner; a prerequisite for; no firm evidence
forerunner
forerunner: standard unit headword
#63 SE
Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.Consuming 25 to 35 percent of their body weight each day, sea otters are not only __________ but highly specialized eaters, organizing themselves into groups that zero in on specific kinds of prey.
答案：A, E　prodigious; voracious
omnivorous
omnivorous: standard unit headword
#64 SE
Recent research runs counter to the long-cherished notion that a small drop in body temperature during and after surgery is either __________ or actually protects the patient by slowing metabolism and reducing the body’s demand for blood and oxygen.Recent research runs counter to the long-cherished notion that a small drop in body temperature during and after surgery is either __________ or actually protects the patient by slowing metabolism and reducing the body’s demand for blood and oxygen.
答案：B, C　immaterial; inconsequential
prophylactic

答案：C, E　vitriolic; acrimonious
droll

Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Throughout United States history there has been (iii) __________—seek utopia—on the frontier, and many space advocates have used that notion to make their case for exploring and settling space.Most advocates of space exploration by the United States would not explicitly associate spaceflight with (i) __________, yet that belief, Launius and McCurdy write, is among the roots of arguments (ii) __________ human spaceflight. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　utopia; prompting; the ideal location for one to better oneself

答案：C, F　stimulated; spurred

答案：B　polymath

答案：C, E　an itinerant; a peripatetic

#70 TC SINGLE
We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of __________.We often regard natural phenomena like rainfall as mysterious and unpredictable; although for short time spans and particular places they appear so, in fact on a truly global scale, nature has been a model of __________.
答案：A　reliability

This journalistic campaign was initially (i)__________, appealing to readers’ sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii)__________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia’s city skirts.In years prior to the Civil War, Philadelphia’s African American press encouraged readers to be vaccinated against the smallpox. This journalistic campaign was initially (i)__________, appealing to readers’ sense of communal duty, and became even more (ii)__________ once the war started, as smallpox outbreaks began to occur on Philadelphia’s city skirts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　hortatory; urgent

Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of __________ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of __________ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie

答案：C　specious

Listening to music in this medium often generates anxiety fulfillment: no sooner has one experience begun than the thought of what else is out there intrudes. Putting an old-fashioned disk and letting it play to the end restores a measure of (ii) __________.

If he feels he is surrounded by economists more (iii)__________ than he is, he may let their assertions, even the dubious ones, stand without challenge.

答案：A　sacrosanct

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize

Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)__________ scientific model is (ii)__________ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)__________ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate

Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.It is plausible to regard a collection of letters spanning youth and old age as (i)__________ of autobiography: the procession of characters who inhabit a life and a chronology of incidents turn up reliably in either form. Yet autobiography, even when ostensibly steeped in candor, tends toward (ii)__________ through later perspectives, afterwords, and second thoughts, whereas letters have an undeniable (iii)__________, offering select glimpses of the fraught and living moment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　an approximation; reconsideration; immediacy

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating

grant: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.Concerned to upend the standard top-down approach to game design, Flanagan calls on game designers to __________ the typical model with one that is less hierarchical.
答案：B, F　supplant; replace
modelupend
model: standard unit headword | upend: standard unit headword

Given the (i) __________ the committees and the (ii) __________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee's conclusions at first glance.Given the (i) __________ the committees and the (ii) __________ nature of its investigation, it would be unreasonable to gainsay the committee's conclusions at first glance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sterling reputation of; exhaustive
exhaustive
exhaustive: standard unit headword
#107 TC TRIPLE
Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　presented; defining features of; propose
model
model: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate __________ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate __________ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.
答案：E　prolixity
preclude
preclude: standard unit headword

Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called __________.Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called __________.
答案：A, C　encyclopedic; exhaustive
exhaustive
exhaustive: standard unit headword
#110 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　umbrage; collectively overthrow
marked
marked: standard unit headword
#111 TC TRIPLE
Medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new research make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that almost require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new research make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that almost require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell
control
control: standard unit headword
#112 SE
In a book that inclines to __________, an epilogue arguing that ballet is dead arrives simply as one more overstatement.In a book that inclines to __________, an epilogue arguing that ballet is dead arrives simply as one more overstatement.
答案：E, F　exaggeration; hyperbole
hyperbole
hyperbole: standard unit headword
#113 TC SINGLE
The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.The notion that prehistoric farming of Micronesia’s Mariana Islands degraded the soils upon which savannas now occur is __________ by soil scientists, who attribute nutrient-poor savanna soils instead to long geological periods of tropical weathering.
答案：C　contradicted
attribute
attribute: standard unit headword

Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)__________ theirs.Parker’s model of human affairs reflects (i)__________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally (ii)__________ premises that her colleagues in the economics department adopt in their work. Accordingly, her conclusions (iii)__________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly from

答案：E　a simplification of
model

The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii) __________ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.Tagore had a sharply defined sense of the (i) __________ of scientific inquiry. The fact that science dealt in statistics and numbers, that its logic was probabilistic, meant that the domain of moral questions (ii) __________ it: moral questions, for Tagore, required certainties, not probabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　limits; lay outside
lie

#117 SE
Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.Though Edmund certainly had a dignified bearing and made a great first impression, those who became acquainted with him soon realized he had an essentially __________ nature.
答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent
essentially
essentially: standard unit headword

This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)__________ them.In the 1980s, many historians sounded urgent calls for (i)__________ in American historical writing, as longer and longer monographs on smaller and smaller subjects were being written—dazzling studies, but pieces of a puzzle no one was putting together. This scholarship was not illuminating the central themes of history but (ii)__________ them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　synthesis; obscuring
dazzling

#119 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
sift

Characteristic of the diplomat’s new book is the __________ relationship between the evidence adduced and the inferences drawn, the footnotes and citations teeming with ambiguity and complexity, while the summary statements are more dogmatic simplicities.Characteristic of the diplomat’s new book is the __________ relationship between the evidence adduced and the inferences drawn, the footnotes and citations teeming with ambiguity and complexity, while the summary statements are more dogmatic simplicities.
答案：C　tenuous

Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to __________ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to __________ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A　superficial

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：C　sycophantic

答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive

Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i)__________ and (ii)__________ every other authors’ writing style.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　stable; distinguishable from

答案：A　deliberate

While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)__________. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)__________ her because of her willingness to (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

答案：A, D　dichotomous; contradictory

Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Collected beetle specimens all contained batrachotoxins, suggesting that these beetles are (iii) __________ by the frogs.The skin of the poison dart frog contains deadly poisons called batrachotoxins. But the (i) __________ of the toxins has remained an enigma, as the frog does not (ii) __________ them. Now an analysis suggests that the melyrid beetle is the source. Blank 2

Warming temperatures have also (iii)__________ of the thistle's fortune.An invasive creeping weed native to the Mediterranean region, Cirsium arvense has been present in Yellowstone National Park at least since the nineteenth century. Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B　stalwart

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　traditional; affinities with; available locally

Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts because (ii)__________ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a scientific report and would be poorly served by (iii)__________.Scientific papers often (i)__________ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　misrepresent; efficiency; a comprehensive historical account

答案：B, E　scant; limited

答案：B　sacrificing rigor

It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unencumbered by; discretionary

But even by these standards, the degree of secrecy surrounding the company's most recent research is (iii)__________.

答案：D　variegated
preclude
preclude: standard unit headword
#161 SE
The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.The dictator’s gleaming military uniform and imperial paraphernalia sharply contrast with the __________ fashion favored by most other contemporary political leaders.
答案：A, B　unostentatious; modest
fashion
fashion: standard unit headword
#162 TC DOUBLE
He was a leader about whom the country's people felt (i) __________: both (ii) __________ and reviling him.He was a leader about whom the country's people felt (i) __________: both (ii) __________ and reviling him.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　ambivalent; adulating
ambivalent
ambivalent: standard unit headword
#163 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
initially
initially: standard unit headword
#164 TC SINGLE
Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.
答案：A　tempers
minimize
minimize: standard unit headword
#165 SE
Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often __________ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.Changes made to ecosystem in order to achieve a goal, such as food production or flood control, often __________ significant unforeseen trade-offs between other important products and service the ecosystems provide.
答案：E, F　yield; engender
control
control: standard unit headword
#166 TC DOUBLE
Even if the merits of the proposal are (i) __________, faculty members may be reluctant to (ii) __________, given their fear of offending the group that champions it.Even if the merits of the proposal are (i) __________, faculty members may be reluctant to (ii) __________, given their fear of offending the group that champions it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　dubious; demur
dubious
dubious: standard unit headword
#167 TC TRIPLE
Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.Firebaugh and Beck contend that economic development improves the overall well-being of people within developing countries. However, other scholars emphasize the (i)__________ of this view, empirically demonstrating that while economic development does in fact contribute to the well-being of the population of developing countries, the magnitude of development's positive effects on well-being has (ii)__________. In other words, these scholars suggest that (iii)__________ economic development and human well-being is taking place in developing countries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　limitation; decreased over time; a decoupling of
magnitude
magnitude: standard unit headword
#168 TC SINGLE
The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.The stars' attitudes toward their glowing press were diametrically opposed, one seeing it as __________ and the other taking the adulation seriously.
答案：D　flummery
press
press: standard unit headword
#169 SE
The brain has become, for many people, __________ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.The brain has become, for many people, __________ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.
答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with
tantamount
tantamount: standard unit headword
#170 TC DOUBLE
Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.Because the book is largely concerned with an examination of various (i) __________ often encountered in contemporary thinking, such as an exaggerated appreciation for meaningless coincidence and a credulous acceptance of pseudoscience, much of the writing has a (ii) __________ quality to it. Nevertheless, it avoids the overly earnest scolding tone common to many such endeavors.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　inadequacies; debunking
pseudoscience
pseudoscience: standard unit headword
#171 TC TRIPLE
Twenty years ago, when the book was first published, most reviewers dismissed the author's predictions as (i)__________. Sometimes people (ii)__________ their errors: those same reviewers today, comparing the predictions with actual events, acknowledge how (iii)__________ the author was.Twenty years ago, when the book was first published, most reviewers dismissed the author's predictions as (i)__________. Sometimes people (ii)__________ their errors: those same reviewers today, comparing the predictions with actual events, acknowledge how (iii)__________ the author was.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　alarmist; recognize; prescient
dismiss
dismiss: standard unit headword
#172 SE
Despite the general __________ of Roman archaeological studies toward the major cities and their monuments, archaeology has contributed much to a better understanding of rural developments in Roman territory.Despite the general __________ of Roman archaeological studies toward the major cities and their monuments, archaeology has contributed much to a better understanding of rural developments in Roman territory.
答案：E, F　bias; orientation
orientation
orientation: standard unit headword
#173 TC SINGLE
Having seen her extravagant predictions of some years ago __________ by events, she is now more cautious: her current predictions are refreshingly free of hyperbole.Having seen her extravagant predictions of some years ago __________ by events, she is now more cautious: her current predictions are refreshingly free of hyperbole.
答案：A　confounded
hyperbole
hyperbole: standard unit headword
#174 TC DOUBLE
The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system--under which the government controls every aspect of construction--creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i) __________ of the building market. Such a complete (ii) __________ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public's interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation
control
control: standard unit headword
#175 TC TRIPLE
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)__________, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)__________, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)__________ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that's worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
initiative
initiative: standard unit headword
#176 SE
It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the __________ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.It may not seem like a big deal for produce distributors to mix together soybeans from different farms, but a growing number of buyers are willing to pay a premium for soybeans the __________ of which is known: they like to know exactly where their food came from.
答案：D, F　origin; provenance
provenance
provenance: standard unit headword
#177 TC SINGLE
Central control of the country's power grid is difficult because of the __________ of the electricity industry-it is made up of hundreds of local networks with separate owners.Central control of the country's power grid is difficult because of the __________ of the electricity industry-it is made up of hundreds of local networks with separate owners.
答案：E　balkanization
control
control: standard unit headword
#178 TC DOUBLE
Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.Computers have become adept in rarefied domains once thought to be uniquely human. However, they simultaneously have (i) __________ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii) __________ such fundamental skills truly are.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　failed to master; impressive
orientation
orientation: standard unit headword
#179 TC TRIPLE
With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools--quantum mechanics and streams of individual photons--to shut out (i) __________. But a wire and a few resistors may (ii) __________ a message as securely, according to physicist who claims to have devised a simple and uncrackable scheme. The idea shows that more (iii) __________ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　prying eyes; convey; mundane
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword

Instead of flooding reporters with updates, the governor's press office dispenses information with the __________ of an eyedropper.Instead of flooding reporters with updates, the governor's press office dispenses information with the __________ of an eyedropper.
答案：A　parsimony
press
press: standard unit headword
#181 SE
A closer examination of the author’s footnotes explains the datedness of his argument: he has ignored most of the recent work in the field, drawing instead mainly on research that is now decidedly __________.A closer examination of the author’s footnotes explains the datedness of his argument: he has ignored most of the recent work in the field, drawing instead mainly on research that is now decidedly __________.
答案：C, F　obsolete; outmoded
draw
draw: standard unit headword
#182 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　disinclination; callow
disinclination

Accordingly, her conclusions (iii) __________ theirs.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　a sanguine; pessimistic; diverge markedly

#184 TC SINGLE

答案：D　grateful
grant

答案：A, E　A trepidation; E apprehension

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　the requisite; badgered him to pursue

#187 TC TRIPLE
Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.Although Uruk in southern Mesopotamia has been (i)__________ as being both the first city and the model for later ones, at least two sites in northern Mesopotamia have yielded clear evidence of urbanization long before the existing evidence from Uruk, and other discoveries indicate that some of the (ii)__________ early urbanism were invented not in southern Mesopotamia but in the north. These findings have led some archaeologists to (iii)__________ a serious reconsideration about when and where the first cities arose.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　established; defining features of; propose
model
model: standard unit headword
#188 TC SINGLE
One of the most appealing things about Sloterdijk's philosophy is his willingness to be __________: he does not attempt to anticipate and to refute all possible objections.One of the most appealing things about Sloterdijk's philosophy is his willingness to be __________: he does not attempt to anticipate and to refute all possible objections.
答案：C　vulnerable
refute
refute: standard unit headword
#189 SE
The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.The research informing Gregory’s book on vegetarianism in Victorian England appears to be __________, with a great deal of revealing detail on display and more than a third of the text taken up with footnotes.
答案：C, E　exhaustive; comprehensive

#190 TC DOUBLE
Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i) __________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii) __________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstroms.Many scholars have argued that the United States Supreme Court usually (i) __________ public opinion in its decisions because it fears that it will (ii) __________ if it does not; when it does depart from public opinion, it whips up political maelstroms.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hews to; lose public support
maelstrom

#191 TC TRIPLE
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
dismiss
dismiss: standard unit headword
#192 SE
Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two do share a family history.Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two do share a family history.
答案：D, E　preclude; rule out

答案：C　recondite

#194 TC DOUBLE
Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i) __________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii) __________ as an extraordinary virtue.Although it is not uncommon for journalists to portray political inexperience on the part of public officials as an (i) __________, it was nevertheless surprising when members of the press treated the new senator's obvious (ii) __________ as an extraordinary virtue.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　impediment; naiveté

#195 TC TRIPLE
People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.People accustomed to thinking that the human lifespan (i) __________ the outer bounds of animal longevity tend to dismiss tales of musket balls being found in the shells of living turtles. Samantha Romney, however, argues that while such stories may be (ii) __________, some turtles do indeed exhibit a phenomenon known as "negligible (iii) __________," showing no signs of aging even as they pass the two-century mark.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　demarcates; apocryphal; senescence
dismiss
dismiss: standard unit headword
#196 SE
During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying __________.During the Renaissance, the use of optical lenses, which were capable of projecting images onto blank canvases, greatly aided artists by allowing them to accurately observe and depict the external world; in other words, these lenses were instrumental in conveying __________.
答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude
verisimilitude

答案：C　opaque to
marked
marked: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic

Blank 1

Blank 3

答案：B　adverse repercussions

答案：C, F　detracting; deprecatory

Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.Psychologists note that in making purchase decisions, consumers often unconsciously use mental shortcuts, such as familiarity with a brand name to (i) __________ routine problems efficiently. Thus, part of what companies aim for developing a brand is very specifically to (ii) __________ consumers' conscious thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：D　porous

答案：C, E　hailed; acclaimed

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　plummeted; decline

Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.Many historians of the ancient world are wary of sounding (i)__________. Write so much as a sentence and the temptation is immediately to (ii)__________ it. Even in cases when the sources for a given event are (iii)__________, uncertainties and discrepancies crop up everywhere.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

#208 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not __________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
precludecircumvent

Although a dedicated reader of the book can __________ a brief chronology of Chappell's life, the book is nevertheless not a real biography of Chappell.Although a dedicated reader of the book can __________ a brief chronology of Chappell's life, the book is nevertheless not a real biography of Chappell.
答案：B, F　obtain; glean

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　emergent; half-formulated

Some climatologists dismiss as (i)__________ the debate among geophysicists over the role of carbon dioxide in global climate change across many millions of years. These climatologists say the evidence of a tie between carbon dioxide and planetary warming over the last few centuries is so (ii)__________ that any longer-term evidence against such a link must somehow be (iii)__________.Some climatologists dismiss as (i)__________ the debate among geophysicists over the role of carbon dioxide in global climate change across many millions of years. These climatologists say the evidence of a tie between carbon dioxide and planetary warming over the last few centuries is so (ii)__________ that any longer-term evidence against such a link must somehow be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted
dismiss

Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.Analysis of the structural features that were thought to __________ kinship between the two species prompted an investigation that dispelled that presumption and revealed that the two share a family history.
答案：D, E　preclude; rule out

#213 TC SINGLE
Scientists have argued not only that the chains of atoms called ladder compounds have __________ theoretical interest but also that studies of such systems can lead to important practical applications.Scientists have argued not only that the chains of atoms called ladder compounds have __________ theoretical interest but also that studies of such systems can lead to important practical applications.
答案：E　intrinsic

#214 TC DOUBLE
The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　metaphor; a real biological feature
cyclical
cyclical: standard unit headword
#215 TC TRIPLE
Sometimes the criteria that are used to categorize nation-states are purely factual: for example, the denotation of a state as a coastal state or an inland state. But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.Sometimes the criteria that are used to categorize nation-states are purely factual: for example, the denotation of a state as a coastal state or an inland state. But most state labels have a predominantly (i)__________ character. Labels such as failed state or democratic state tend to be accepted only by those who (ii)__________ the assumptions that (iii)__________ such a marker.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　evaluative; share; constitute the basis of
marker
marker: standard unit headword
#216 TC DOUBLE
Some people (i) __________ translations of great literary works--especially those insistent on a literal translation for whom no rendering is ever (ii) __________ enough.Some people (i) __________ translations of great literary works--especially those insistent on a literal translation for whom no rendering is ever (ii) __________ enough.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　dismiss; faithful
dismiss

答案：A　specious
ambivalent
ambivalent: standard unit headword

Since the data we have analyzed are so __________, it would be hazardous to draw a definite conclusion.Since the data we have analyzed are so __________, it would be hazardous to draw a definite conclusion.
答案：C, D　meager; paltry
draw
draw: standard unit headword
#219 TC TRIPLE
Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.Belief in the harmful effects of the drug has all the hallmarks of a viewpoint that is not (i) __________ even though the notion that the drug has negative consequences has been (ii) __________ multiple epidemiological studies over the past decade. Yet the true believers have (iii) __________, critiquing each new study that challenges their views and continuing to lean on supporting research that was retracted long ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amenable to refutation; upended by; persisted
upend
upend: standard unit headword

The film was a __________: its elements were assembled more or less haphazardly from a dozen of different sources.The film was a __________: its elements were assembled more or less haphazardly from a dozen of different sources.
答案：C　pastiche
chronicle
chronicle: standard unit headword
#221 SE
With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve __________ just as well as truth.With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve __________ just as well as truth.
答案：D, E　disinformation; mendacity

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　precluded; unnatural

Specifically, the pulsar's periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) __________ rather than--as it turned out to be--the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.When pulsars were first discovered, some of the astrophysicists involved briefly (i) __________ these types of stars might be (ii) __________ extraterrestrial intelligence. Specifically, the pulsar's periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) __________ rather than--as it turned out to be--the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code

答案：C　lucidity

答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

People who watch what they eat probably exercise more, avoid smoking, and follow other behaviors (iii) __________ good health. Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.In general, observational studies tend to (i) __________ the effect of diet on overall health because a healthy diet is often (ii) __________ a healthy lifestyle. People who watch what they eat probably exercise more, avoid smoking, and follow other behaviors (iii) __________ good health. Any of those behaviors might also help explain why these people have lower disease rates.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　overstate; a marker for; linked to

But this agricultural archive is (i) __________ as a result of war, storms, scant money, and bad management, particularly in the world's most (ii) __________ places.

答案：D　linked

Although this pattern is (i)__________ in that it confirms warnings about our own possibly dire future, it is also somehow (ii)__________ as it sets forth a context for today's turbulent weather as (iii)__________ in a natural, cyclical process.Climate change is posited to be a frequent factor in mass extinctions by some scientists who speculate that minor climate shifts can trigger a catastrophic cascade of effects. 答案：A, E, I　alarming; reassuring; unexceptional

There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.There is little question that in primates there is (i) __________ between monogamous mating partnerships and extensive male care of infants. Nevertheless, that does not mean male care is (ii) __________ situations in which females mate with more than one male; studies of savanna baboons show that male care certainly can occur in nonmonogamous systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　an association; precluded by
preclude
preclude: standard unit headword
#270 TC TRIPLE
According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.According to linguists and cognitive scientists, the human mind specializes in producing analogies. Part of the reason cognitive scientists believe analogy is a (i) __________ cognitive process is that drawing analogies requires some sort of similarity mapping of one concept onto another, and similarity mapping is a component of the inductive thinking we depend on. Another argument for the (ii) __________ of analogical reasoning in human reasoning is that metaphors, which are linguistically embedded analogies, are (iii) __________ language.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　fundamental; centrality; pervasive in
draw
draw: standard unit headword
#271 SE
The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.The scientific study of laughter faces a frustrating paradox: ideal experiments take place in rigidly controlled environments with all but the most salient variables eliminated, but such sterile settings are often __________ laughter.
答案：A, D　inimical to; adverse to
control
control: standard unit headword
#272 TC TRIPLE
According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are (i) __________ the challenge of fostering an adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with, core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a (ii) __________ ethics, thus creating an (iii) __________ audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable
cynical
cynical: standard unit headword
#273 TC SINGLE
Those who hypothesize former volcanoes on Ceres must account for the volcanoes' __________: because Ceres lacks an atmosphere, the erosion processes that wear down Earth's mountains don't occur on the dwarf planet.Those who hypothesize former volcanoes on Ceres must account for the volcanoes' __________: because Ceres lacks an atmosphere, the erosion processes that wear down Earth's mountains don't occur on the dwarf planet.
答案：E　disappearance
magnitude
magnitude: standard unit headword
#274 TC DOUBLE
At the heart of present-day studies computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i) __________ and (ii) __________ every other author's writing style.At the heart of present-day studies computational authorship attribution lies the hypothesis that each author has so personal a writing style that it can function as stylistic DNA. Any given author style, in other words, is regarded as (i) __________ and (ii) __________ every other author's writing style.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　stable; distinguishable from
lie
lie: standard unit headword

Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.Hsu is __________ by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently flouted established fashion trends.
答案：A, C　independent; freewheeling
fashion
fashion: standard unit headword
#276 SE
Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.Because urban soils are formed from diverse, often non-soil, materials and have gone through variable periods of development, the normal heterogeneity encountered in wetland soils may be greatly __________ in urban wetlands.
答案：E, F　magnified; intensified
magnify
magnify: standard unit headword

答案：C　involves a loss of
preclude

Blank 1
Blank 2

Unlike news reports, theater isn't expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i) __________, the form, by nature, traffics in (ii) __________. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what's real and what's (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented

Since eventually the initial conditions (i) __________ the evolving planet, this approach is more (ii) __________ for mature planets than it is for young planets.Standard thermal evolution models of giant planets employ initial conditions that are, to some extent, arbitrarily chosen, selected more for computational expediency than for physical accuracy. Since eventually the initial conditions (i) __________ the evolving planet, this approach is more (ii) __________ for mature planets than it is for young planets.
Blank 1

To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i) __________, mathematicians are (ii) __________ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii) __________. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　reliable; skeptical regarding; appear to work
