#284 TC SINGLE
The __________ effectiveness of lithium as a mood-altering drug for both manic and depressive states cannot be completely explained; most mood-altering drugs can only be used to modify one state or the other.The __________ effectiveness of lithium as a mood-altering drug for both manic and depressive states cannot be completely explained; most mood-altering drugs can only be used to modify one state or the other.
答案：D　paradoxical
dubious

#285 TC DOUBLE
The reason geological activity on Mars is so (i) __________ while on Earth it remains (ii) __________ is that Earth's diameter is about twice the size of Mars. Because Mars is smaller than Earth, it cooled faster, and therefore it lost the interrial heat that drives tectonic activity.The reason geological activity on Mars is so (i) __________ while on Earth it remains (ii) __________ is that Earth's diameter is about twice the size of Mars. Because Mars is smaller than Earth, it cooled faster, and therefore it lost the interrial heat that drives tectonic activity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　moribund; vital
mar
mar: standard unit headword
#286 SE
Mathematics is concerned with pattern and order, and since we could not exist in a universe with neither pattern nor order, the fact that we live in a world that is mathematical is not __________.Mathematics is concerned with pattern and order, and since we could not exist in a universe with neither pattern nor order, the fact that we live in a world that is mathematical is not __________.
答案：A, F　a puzzle; an enigma
enigma

The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.The authors considered how it can be that the fashion industry continues to (i) __________ when there are no laws that prevent one designer or company from copying another designer's original idea. They came to the conclusion that the fashion industry (ii) __________ the piracy of new designs: design copying contributes to a process of induced obsolescence by diffusing new designs into the mainstream, causing them to lose their insider appeal. A market for new designs is thus (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　prosper; benefits from; perpetuated
fashion
fashion: standard unit headword

答案：D　an adjunct to

答案：A, E　an enigma; a mystery

#290 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

答案：D　uncomfortable precedent

答案：C, E　eradicate; extirpate

The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as (i) __________ as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) __________ to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (iii) __________ by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

#296 SE
Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.
答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate
repudiate

But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.The notion of popular science or of the public understanding of science is very much a concern in today's world, where we can see (i) __________ between science and nonscience. But in the nineteenth century, there was still an enviable mixing and cross-fertilization between seemingly (ii) __________ subjects, and there was no (iii) __________ what counted as science, let alone how a public understanding might differ from any other sort of understanding.

With Easterners still (i) __________ the representation of the West, western writers had to publish in the East in order to succeed, despite a (ii) __________ literary scene in many western cities. Not surprisingly, this editor urged Westerners to (iii) __________ representations of the West created by Easterners.In 1859 the editor of one newspaper in the western United States lamented that "the popular idea of Western Literature" consisted of such cliched topics as "log cabins, rough speech, dare-devil boldness, bear-hunting and cornhusking." Not surprisingly, this editor urged Westerners to (iii) __________ representations of the West created by Easterners.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　controlling; burgeoning; reject

答案：B, C　unwarranted; inappropriate

The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　complex; connection to; more direct

But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound

Blank 2

Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i) __________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii) __________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.The political theorist argues that passing laws to (i) __________ the state in order to preclude abuse of its citizenry sounds appealing and even necessary, but doing so can (ii) __________ the state's ability to protect its citizens from both external and internal aggression. Most citizens want a compromise: a state strong enough to protect them but not so strong that it can harm them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　constrain; vitiate
preclude

#316 SE
Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.Although the author affects a breezy, ironic tone in much of his writing, his __________ gives way to palpable sadness when he writes about his experiences during the First World War.
答案：C, D　insouciance; detachment

#317 TC TRIPLE
Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.Models of the interior evolution of Venus suggest that the planet (i) __________ periods of relative (ii) __________ and periods of instability and rapid surface overturn. In other words, Venus may "freak out" occasionally, getting rid of its internal heat in great planetwide (iii) __________ of activity, rather than in the steadier cycling of lithospheric plates that occurs on Earth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　oscillates between; quiescence; paroxysms
model
model: standard unit headword
#318 TC DOUBLE
Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.Scottish writer Mary Brunton's novels Self-control (1811) and Discipline (1814) are undeniably (i) __________: her plots, involving virtue rewarded and excess avoided, (ii) __________ cautionary tales, though the psychological analysis that pervades the books saves them from blandness and sentimentality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　didactic; amount to
control
control: standard unit headword
#319 SE
The __________ of cosmic rays remains mysterious because their paths are bent by the galaxy's magnetic field--meaning that the directions they come from do not point to whatever created them.The __________ of cosmic rays remains mysterious because their paths are bent by the galaxy's magnetic field--meaning that the directions they come from do not point to whatever created them.
答案：B, E　origin; provenance
provenance
provenance: standard unit headword
#320 TC TRIPLE
Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.Biographies tumble off the presses in profusion these days, a (i) __________ linked, for better or worse, to the current obsession with celebrity. Some critics complain that biographers too often (ii) __________ the messy reality of a life, and it's true that lesser authors simply glorify or, more often, vilify their subjects. But good biographers are far more respectful of (iii) __________: the best see not one monolithic truth about a person but many overlapping truths.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　boom; impose a specious coherence on; complexity
press
press: standard unit headword
#321 TC DOUBLE
In contrast with more traditional, deterministic models, George Ellis' model of an evolving block universe describes a universe with (i) __________ future, seemingly (ii) __________ a law-governed universe in which past states dictate future states.In contrast with more traditional, deterministic models, George Ellis' model of an evolving block universe describes a universe with (i) __________ future, seemingly (ii) __________ a law-governed universe in which past states dictate future states.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an open; incompatible with
model
model: standard unit headword
#322 SE
Growing up far from China, Sienna Parulis-Cook had read tales of mooncake that led her to dismiss the palm-sized delicacy as __________, but in Beijing, she discovered that mooncakes have modernized considerably in a generation.Growing up far from China, Sienna Parulis-Cook had read tales of mooncake that led her to dismiss the palm-sized delicacy as __________, but in Beijing, she discovered that mooncakes have modernized considerably in a generation.
答案：A, E　antiquated; old-fashioned
fashionold-fashioneddismiss
fashion: standard unit headword | old-fashioned: standard unit headword | dismiss: standard unit headword
#323 TC TRIPLE
When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.When reviewing a book that advertises lying as its subject, the critic has a duty to screen the title for possible (i) __________, and it is therefore necessary to report that The Concise Book of Lying is not concise. If the book were making a case for deception, the mismatch might not matter: a (ii) __________ title might suit a book designed to disabuse us of our respect for (iii) __________. But the book is not an apology for lying: it is on the side of truth.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　ambiguity; misleading; veracity
lie
lie: standard unit headword
#324 TC DOUBLE
Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　indirect; an inference
lie
lie: standard unit headword
#325 SE
The clients initially wanted to declare bankruptcy, but their lawyers were reluctant, noting that bankruptcy can often be a __________ process.The clients initially wanted to declare bankruptcy, but their lawyers were reluctant, noting that bankruptcy can often be a __________ process.
答案：A, F　parlous; risky
initially
initially: standard unit headword
#326 TC TRIPLE
The (i)__________ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.The (i)__________ quality of much contemporary drawing may be attributable to the use of photography as a drawing shortcut. Photography (ii)__________ modern arts, but when it is used as a tracing tool in order to (iii)__________ the difficulties of achieving correct proportion, the resulting art often feels static and lifeless.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　inert; wonderfully enriched; circumvent

draw: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword

Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.Though extremely (i) __________ about her own plans, she allowed her associates no such (ii) __________ and was constantly badgering them for information about what they intended to do next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　reticent; discretion
badger
badger: standard unit headword
#328 SE
Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.Although it is premature to say whether nanomotors would __________ conventional methods, Wang believes that the most promising applications for nanomotors lie in fast drug delivery or low-cost cleanup of environmental pollutants.
答案：D, E　supplant; supersede
lie
lie: standard unit headword
#329 TC TRIPLE
Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.Many popular musicians have (i)__________ new digital technologies that allow them unprecedented control over their music. These musicians use computers to (ii)__________ and modify their songs, resulting in a level of musical precision often unattainable naturally. Of course, though, as is often the case with new technologies, some traditionalists (iii)__________ these developments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　incorporated; recast; balk at

Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.Some norms governing authors' dedications of their works are (i) __________ the norms governing ordinary speech (it may be wrong to lie, and thus wrong to lie in a dedication), but other norms are (ii) __________ dedications. For example, one ought not dedicate to a worthy recipient an object that one feels is devoid of value.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　indistinct from; particular to

lie: standard unit headword
#331 SE
Some bacteria utilize a mechanism by which they can __________: these bacteria use small, diffusible chemical signals to gauge the density of their population and initiate group-beneficial behaviors at high population densities.Some bacteria utilize a mechanism by which they can __________: these bacteria use small, diffusible chemical signals to gauge the density of their population and initiate group-beneficial behaviors at high population densities.
答案：A, E　cooperate; collaborate

#332 TC DOUBLE
In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.

Blank 2
答案：B, D　distractions; minimize their effect

If you wish to make a novel (i)__________ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)__________ received opinion, which can (iii)__________ a nascent idea before it can develop.If you wish to make a novel (i)__________ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)__________ received opinion, which can (iii)__________ a nascent idea before it can develop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　contribution to; oppressive influence of; quash

#334 SE
The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market's sometimes destructive vicissitudes being __________ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.
答案：A, E　tempered; mitigated

#335 TC TRIPLE
The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)__________ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.The historian of ancient science Otto Neugebauer concluded that Babylonian astronomical texts are (i)__________ because everything has been eliminated from the astronomy except observations and the mathematical consequences of an initial hypothesis about the fundamental character of the astronomical movements. This judgment cohered with the high level of mathematical theory, which (ii)__________ mathematical computation together with empirical observation as (iii)__________ of science and denied any role to speculative hypotheses of a strongly theoretical nature.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　scientific; admitted; necessary characteristic
repudiate
repudiate: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：C, D　purports; old-fashioned

#341 TC TRIPLE
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)__________: the witness to that is the (ii)__________ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)__________, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill
provenance
provenance: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　repudiate; recant

In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.In a 1952 essay, Thomas Kuhn argued that Robert Boyle's corpuscular philosophy was purely mechanistic. Kuhn, however, is not (i) __________ in attributing a strict Cartesian mechanism to Boyle. In fact, this view of Boyle (ii) __________ Fontenelle, who, in his History of the Royal Academy of Sciences of 1733, identifies corpuscularism with Cartesian mechanism.

Blank 2
答案：C, E　unique; can be traced to

For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we've stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)__________ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)__________ notion recently has (iii)__________ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book's value had he more effectively (ii)__________ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy; understandably, space in such a book is (i)__________, and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. #364 TC DOUBLE
Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.Fashion designer Elsa Schiaparelli was just nineteen years old when Futurism erupted, but she later heeded that violent, belligerent art movement's call in the (i) __________ of her broad-shouldered suits, the rawness of her furs and embroidery, and her (ii) __________ attitude, which her contemporaries described as "hard chic," toward any simpering or mincing in fashion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　swagger; pugnacious
fashion
fashion: standard unit headword
#365 TC TRIPLE
Much of the newspaper's readership found it hard to (i)__________ the allegations of venality laid against the superintendent, since these readers were still (ii)__________ the superintendent's reputation for (iii)__________. The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.Much of the newspaper's readership found it hard to (i)__________ the allegations of venality laid against the superintendent, since these readers were still (ii)__________ the superintendent's reputation for (iii)__________. The superintendent's subsequent vindication will have come as no surprise to them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　credit; swayed by; probity
dismiss
dismiss: standard unit headword
#366 SE
The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.The concept of increasing complexity of organisms has __________ history among evolutionary biologists, and yet many laypeople would unhesitatingly say that the pattern applies to the history of life on Earth.
答案：D, F　a contentious; a fraught
fraughtlaypeople
fraught: standard unit headword | laypeople: standard unit headword
#367 TC DOUBLE
In clinical research into new treatments or the causal factors of diseases, randomized, double-blind controlled clinical trials are seen as (i) __________ but, given their (ii) __________, often are not feasible.In clinical research into new treatments or the causal factors of diseases, randomized, double-blind controlled clinical trials are seen as (i) __________ but, given their (ii) __________, often are not feasible.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　the gold standard; expense
control
control: standard unit headword
#368 TC TRIPLE
Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.Persian prose writers from the second half of the twelfth century onward were characteristically (i)__________ the literary form of their works, a fact that some scholars have perceived as a derogation of those works' content. It may be better interpreted as (ii)__________ the development of an awareness of authorship, for the awareness of authorship (iii)__________ the awareness of form.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　preoccupied with; an indication of; evolves through
ambivalent
ambivalent: standard unit headword
#369 TC DOUBLE
Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i) __________ findings, but even among those findings that managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii) __________ a very restricted class of cases.Not only is the field of behavioral genetics strewn with (i) __________ findings, but even among those findings that managed to survive, many have turned out to be (ii) __________ a very restricted class of cases.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　repudiated; germane to
repudiaterefute
repudiate: standard unit headword | refute: standard unit headword
#370 SE
Margaret Oliphant's literary output was __________: it included almost 100 novels, 50 short stories, 25 nonfictional works, and in the region of 400 articles published in the periodical press.Margaret Oliphant's literary output was __________: it included almost 100 novels, 50 short stories, 25 nonfictional works, and in the region of 400 articles published in the periodical press.
答案：C, D　ample; prodigious
press

#371 TC TRIPLE
Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.Anthony Kenny states that although his A New History of Western Philosophy is intended for undergraduates, he aimed to write it in a lighthearted manner that will give (i)__________ to those who read the history "not for curricular purposes but for their own enjoyment". His book is certainly enlightening, and although the bibliography includes technical works liable to (ii)__________ the newcomer, the work is written at a level that generally (iii)__________ between elementary and advanced.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　pleasure; baffle; strikes a good balance
initiate
initiate: standard unit headword
#372 SE
Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.Edric's imagination has always been drawn to the peripheral, to characters who are set apart or seeking a geography to match their sense of spiritual __________.
答案：A, B　exile; separation
draw
draw: standard unit headword
#373 TC DOUBLE
The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　skeptical of; shun

It is only recently that emotion has attracted any substantial scholarly attention, with historians lagging behind anthropologists, sociologists, and philosophers in their willingness to (i)__________ emotion as a subject worthy of scholarly attention. In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)__________ analysis - a strictly private matter - and therefore (iii)__________ social life and the stuff of research.It is only recently that emotion has attracted any substantial scholarly attention, with historians lagging behind anthropologists, sociologists, and philosophers in their willingness to (i)__________ emotion as a subject worthy of scholarly attention. In the past, scholars viewed emotion as a natural and essential force that (ii)__________ analysis - a strictly private matter - and therefore (iii)__________ social life and the stuff of research.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　recognize; defied; extraneous to

repudiate: standard unit headword
#375 SE
Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.Padilla is a contrarian by nature, so it is no surprise that the clothes she designs have consistently __________ established fashion trends.
答案：B, F　challenged; flouted

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　longed for; shunned

#377 TC TRIPLE
The scientist (i)__________ that the now familiar term "global warming" is (ii)__________, arguing that the atmospheric buildup of long-lived greenhouse gases is setting in motion centuries of shifts in climate patterns, coastlines, water resources, and ecosystems - hardly (iii)__________ one would describe with a gentle word like "warming".The scientist (i)__________ that the now familiar term "global warming" is (ii)__________, arguing that the atmospheric buildup of long-lived greenhouse gases is setting in motion centuries of shifts in climate patterns, coastlines, water resources, and ecosystems - hardly (iii)__________ one would describe with a gentle word like "warming".
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　complained; a misnomer; a transformation

enigma: standard unit headword
#378 SE
By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.By avoiding hyperbole in her definitive account of one of the most influential but controversial banks of the modern era, Hurst shows herself to be a __________ author.
答案：A, D　judicious; sensible
hyperbole
hyperbole: standard unit headword
#379 TC DOUBLE
The most (i) __________ weather phenomenon one might observe on Mars is a dust storm. No one knows when a dust storm might hit or how severe it will be, but the worst such storms can (ii) __________ the planet's entire surface in a cloud of yellow dust.The most (i) __________ weather phenomenon one might observe on Mars is a dust storm. No one knows when a dust storm might hit or how severe it will be, but the worst such storms can (ii) __________ the planet's entire surface in a cloud of yellow dust.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　dramatic; obscure
mar

#380 TC TRIPLE
It is inevitable that ongoing research presupposes some accepted science as a principle. Usually no one even notices the implication that the accepted principle is being (i)__________ unless there emerges (ii)__________ that turns out to be sufficiently (iii)__________ that the assumptions underlying the research come to be considered.It is inevitable that ongoing research presupposes some accepted science as a principle. Usually no one even notices the implication that the accepted principle is being (i)__________ unless there emerges (ii)__________ that turns out to be sufficiently (iii)__________ that the assumptions underlying the research come to be considered.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　tested; an anomaly; recalcitrant

Researchers' use of charcoal particles in soil as a marker for human arrival in prehistoric North America is __________: fire was a part of the continent's natural ecology long before humans appeared.Researchers' use of charcoal particles in soil as a marker for human arrival in prehistoric North America is __________: fire was a part of the continent's natural ecology long before humans appeared.
答案：A, F　unwise; misguided
marker

#382 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　best suited for; assumes

#383 TC TRIPLE
The mind has a tendency to (i)__________ abstractions, so that one might envision the ego existing somewhere physically in the brain, but Freud (ii)__________ such a physical structure: rather, he (iii)__________.The mind has a tendency to (i)__________ abstractions, so that one might envision the ego existing somewhere physically in the brain, but Freud (ii)__________ such a physical structure: rather, he (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　hypostatize; did not posit; regarded it as a process

In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.In his article, Bazerman highlights Edison's __________ manipulation of the press, including gifts of stock to friendly reporters.
答案：D, F　brazen; barefaced

#385 TC DOUBLE

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　paramount in; germane

#386 TC TRIPLE
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relativity have been astonishingly (i) __________ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii) __________ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii) __________, a fact that has disallowed theorists--at least so far--from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each other

laypeople: standard unit headword
#387 SE
The beauty of popular fiction always lies in some __________ conviction, a writer's faith in courage, honor, integrity, love, whatever--a faith that's impossible to fake, difficult even to acquire.The beauty of popular fiction always lies in some __________ conviction, a writer's faith in courage, honor, integrity, love, whatever--a faith that's impossible to fake, difficult even to acquire.
答案：C, F　unreserved; wholehearted

The author of this biography gives an accurate and (i)__________ account of the subject’s life story, but all of this carefully assembled detail fails to compensate for the general lack of (ii)__________ in her writing.The author of this biography gives an accurate and (i)__________ account of the subject’s life story, but all of this carefully assembled detail fails to compensate for the general lack of (ii)__________ in her writing.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　selective; vivacity

#389 TC TRIPLE
If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) __________ challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) __________ papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) __________ throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.If a figure such as the editor and writer Pauline Hopkins presents (i) __________ challenges to a would-be biographer because of her confounding periods of science, largely unknown personal life, and (ii) __________ papers, the problems posed by John Edward Bruce are of the opposite kind: he published (iii) __________ throughout his life, had an extensive correspondence, and left a huge trove of materials at his death.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　colossal; meager; indefatigably
lucid
lucid: standard unit headword
#390 SE
Initially portrayed as resistant to change, the protagonist gradually becomes more open-minded; nevertheless, she remains somewhat __________.Initially portrayed as resistant to change, the protagonist gradually becomes more open-minded; nevertheless, she remains somewhat __________.
答案：A, C　cautious; circumspect
initially
initially: standard unit headword
#391 TC DOUBLE
Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.Although the rich history of filmed versions of Shakespeare’s plays reaches back to the early twentieth century, filmmakers still appear to (i)__________. They typically seek to (ii)__________ their work by claiming that film techniques can improve upon Shakespeare’s text and on the conditions of theatrical performance, and yet such claims, asserted too strenuously, can seem defensive.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　doubt their own legitimacy; validate
minimize

Why doesn't everyone love sherry, they asked, celebrating it as the most (iii) __________ wine in the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　lamented; indifference to; underappreciated

答案：A, F　asserts; maintains

#394 TC DOUBLE

Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism

#395 TC TRIPLE
Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　presence; function as a proxy for; supporting
repudiate
repudiate: standard unit headword

When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.When studying the ancient Greek astronomers, Copernicus realized that despite the intrinsic beauty of many of their arguments, the ancients often made claims that __________ logic.
答案：E, F　defied; disregarded

Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate

答案：B, E　a hodgepodge; a patchwork

#400 TC DOUBLE
Early in the development of ice age theories, physicists identified the precession (slow gyration) and wobbling of Earth’s spin axis as the likely drivers of the ice ages, but geologists (i)__________, and even the painstaking work done in the 1920s and 1930s by Mihution Mplankovitch failed to (ii)__________ of many of the day’s geological experts.Early in the development of ice age theories, physicists identified the precession (slow gyration) and wobbling of Earth’s spin axis as the likely drivers of the ice ages, but geologists (i)__________, and even the painstaking work done in the 1920s and 1930s by Mihution Mplankovitch failed to (ii)__________ of many of the day’s geological experts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　demurred; assuage the qualms

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed

答案：C, D　brook; tolerate

Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)__________ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)__________ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)__________ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)__________ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　a significant struggle; entails

Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) __________ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) __________ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) __________ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror

答案：A, F　preserving; safeguarding

Blank 1
Blank 2

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　wide-ranging; mimic

Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.Taruskin's history of Western music, (i) __________ its remarkable length, makes no claims to comprehensiveness. Taruskin is punctilious, and justifiably proud, in emphasizing that his book is a work of history, not a survey of the repertory. In fact, he (ii) __________ criticism on grounds of omission by cheerfully admitting he has (iii) __________ such side-branch composers as Vaughan Williams, who maintained the British symphonic tradition into the mid-twentieth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　despite; disarms; ignored

refute: standard unit headword

The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very __________ subject.The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very __________ subject.
答案：A, C　contentious; controversial
mundane

Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii) __________ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.In aquatic environments, the herbicide atrazine is more likely to (i) __________ developing amphibians when it is highly diluted than when it is much more concentrated, a new study suggests. Although counterintuitive, the finding is (ii) __________ some past research on atrazine and studies showing that other hormonally active compounds are most damaging at trace concentrations.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　kill; consistent with
circumvent
circumvent: standard unit headword
#413 TC TRIPLE
Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.Because popular culture often portrays lawyers as shady and scientists as eccentric, we might expect cinematic depictions of scientists employed as expert witnesses in court cases to reflect badly on both law and science. Yet science and scientists rarely (i) __________ in movies involving trials. When a scientist is negatively portrayed, it is often because the legal process is represented as having (ii) __________ science; when a scientist is positively portrayed, it is often because science is represented as superior to law, as (iii) __________ law's rhetorical and institutional instabilities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　suffer reputational losses; tainted; a counterweight to
emulate
emulate: standard unit headword
#414 SE
Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain __________ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain __________ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.
答案：A, E　feigned; false
dubious
dubious: standard unit headword

While people complain about their hectic lives and demanding schedules, one might be justified in suspecting that they are being somewhat (i) __________: compulsive busyness seems to be, for many, a source of (ii) __________.While people complain about their hectic lives and demanding schedules, one might be justified in suspecting that they are being somewhat (i) __________: compulsive busyness seems to be, for many, a source of (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　disingenuous; pride
dilatory
dilatory: standard unit headword
#416 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)__________ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)__________, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)__________ what has become the establish view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
modelinitiallycircumvent
model: standard unit headword | initially: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　complicated; knotty

mundane: standard unit headword

On the contrary, we find ourselves wondering whether his genius wasn't (ii) __________ in some mysterious way by his mood.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　hampered; served

Blank 2
答案：A, F　immersed in; radicalism

Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)__________ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)__________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)__________ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.Given children’s active fantasy lives, one might think of truthfulness as a young child’s (i)__________ virtue. It turns out that lying is the more (ii)__________ skill. A child who is going to lie must recognize the truth, intellectually conceive of an alternate reality, and be able to convincingly sell that new reality to someone else. Therefore, lying (iii)__________ both cognitive development and social skills that honesty simply doesn’t require.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　an acquired; advanced; demands

答案：D, F　a puzzle; a conundrum

Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking

Blank 2

hyperbole: standard unit headword
#467 TC DOUBLE
Many of the unusual behaviors attributed to crows--such as drinking coffee or presenting gifts to people who feed them--are based on (i) __________ and therefore fall into the category of (ii) __________ rather than science.Many of the unusual behaviors attributed to crows--such as drinking coffee or presenting gifts to people who feed them--are based on (i) __________ and therefore fall into the category of (ii) __________ rather than science.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　secondhand testimony; anecdote
controlattribute
control: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword
#468 SE
Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal __________ per se.
答案：A, F　complexity; intricacy
magnitude
magnitude: standard unit headword
#469 TC DOUBLE
Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i) __________ and almost (ii) __________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i) __________ and almost (ii) __________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for
ceremonialdetriment
ceremonial: standard unit headword | detriment: standard unit headword
#470 SE
Scholars viewed the ancient Antikythera mechanism as __________ until scientific imaging methods revealed inscriptions on it showing that its purpose was to calculate the locations and movements of the stars, Moon, and planets.Scholars viewed the ancient Antikythera mechanism as __________ until scientific imaging methods revealed inscriptions on it showing that its purpose was to calculate the locations and movements of the stars, Moon, and planets.
答案：B, E　cryptic; enigmatic
ceremonial
ceremonial: standard unit headword
#471 TC DOUBLE
Research into butterfly wings could have (i) __________ implications, since knowledge of their optical and thermal properties may be (ii) __________ controlling the behavior of computer chips, which likewise consist of finely structured thin films.Research into butterfly wings could have (i) __________ implications, since knowledge of their optical and thermal properties may be (ii) __________ controlling the behavior of computer chips, which likewise consist of finely structured thin films.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　technological; germane to
controltantamount
control: standard unit headword | tantamount: standard unit headword
#472 SE
Because they require abstraction and generalization, many theories end up __________ practical relevance as they fail to capture the richness and complexity of phenomena encountered in real settings.Because they require abstraction and generalization, many theories end up __________ practical relevance as they fail to capture the richness and complexity of phenomena encountered in real settings.
答案：A, E　lacking; wanting
repudiate
repudiate: standard unit headword
#473 TC DOUBLE
As a longtime fan of the fashion photography Web site, I wasn't sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i) __________ that implies. What I've always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii) __________: they are disposable snapshots of what's going on in the world of style right now.As a longtime fan of the fashion photography Web site, I wasn't sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i) __________ that implies. What I've always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii) __________: they are disposable snapshots of what's going on in the world of style right now.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　permanency; ephemeral
fashionmundane
fashion: standard unit headword | mundane: standard unit headword
#474 SE
Hugh Trevor-Roper's series of essays on the Puritan Revolution are considered to be the __________ of the genre: though these essays have exerted a welcome influence on the work of many subsequent historians, their quality has never been equaled.Hugh Trevor-Roper's series of essays on the Puritan Revolution are considered to be the __________ of the genre: though these essays have exerted a welcome influence on the work of many subsequent historians, their quality has never been equaled.
答案：B, D　apogee; acme
forerunner
forerunner: standard unit headword
#475 TC DOUBLE
There is a somewhat (i) __________ trend emerging in property law today: though property law historically has been used to legitimize the conquest of indigenous lands, indigenous groups worldwide are now (ii) __________ this same body of law to lay claim to their own cultural resources.There is a somewhat (i) __________ trend emerging in property law today: though property law historically has been used to legitimize the conquest of indigenous lands, indigenous groups worldwide are now (ii) __________ this same body of law to lay claim to their own cultural resources.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　ironic; employing
liecircumvent
lie: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword
#476 SE
The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.The wealth of insightful and challenging articles found in the journal is astounding in a world where mainstream publications increasingly focus on the most __________ of topics.
答案：A, C　hackneyed; pedestrian
fashionable
fashionable: standard unit headword
#477 TC DOUBLE
Professor Redfield describes in his recent book publishing as dark as it is deep, even as one hidebound industry. Indeed, it is facing a profound change in its product model, but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunities offered by technological change.Professor Redfield describes in his recent book publishing as dark as it is deep, even as one hidebound industry. Indeed, it is facing a profound change in its product model, but is so (i) __________ its past as to be (ii) __________ opportunities offered by technological change.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　encumbered by; unable to seize

model: standard unit headword | circumvent: standard unit headword
#478 SE
The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.The artist reworked certain of his etchings in order to make the scenes they depict more logical, yet, paradoxically, the pictorial space seems to become more __________ the more realistically it is defined.
答案：D, F　fanciful; chimerical
lucid
lucid: standard unit headword
#479 TC DOUBLE
To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.To understand what accounts for the durability of the Chinese empire, we must first (i) __________ of deterministic explanations attributing it to an unusually convenient topographic setting. The empire's terrain, divided into natural enclaves by mountain ranges and huge rivers, was as (ii) __________ the emergence of small independent polities as any other part of the world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　question the adequacy; conducive to
dismissattribute
dismiss: standard unit headword | attribute: standard unit headword
#480 SE
At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.At the turn of the twentieth century, the status of dinosaurs changed dramatically in the United States; fascination with them, previously __________ passion, became widespread.
答案：A, F　an esoteric; an arcane
mundane
mundane: standard unit headword
#481 SE
The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.The simplicity of Ann Hamilton's sculpture, a single drop of water snaking its way down a vast white wall, __________ the elaborate technical apparatus hidden beneath its surface.
答案：C, D　belied; contradicted
minimize
minimize: standard unit headword
#482 SE
Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.Leaders within the Native American culture known as Fort Ancient differed from those of the neighboring Mississippian groups, whose authority was often __________; indeed, one expert has described the Fort Ancient society as intensely egalitarian.
答案：D, F　coercive; imperious
dubious
dubious: standard unit headword
#483 SE
Neuroscientists are discovering that our sensory systems are much more interconnected than previously thought: even in ordinary circumstances, our senses can __________ one another in extraordinary ways.Neuroscientists are discovering that our sensory systems are much more interconnected than previously thought: even in ordinary circumstances, our senses can __________ one another in extraordinary ways.
答案：C, F　abet; support
circumvent
circumvent: standard unit headword
#484 SE
A key characteristic in the author's presentation of his character's dreary life is __________: paragraphs are short, sentences are laconic, patterns of repetition and circularity are evoked by means of symbolic shorthand.A key characteristic in the author's presentation of his character's dreary life is __________: paragraphs are short, sentences are laconic, patterns of repetition and circularity are evoked by means of symbolic shorthand.
答案：B, D　economy; concision
hyperbole
hyperbole: standard unit headword
#485 SE
Offscreen, actor Madhur Jaffrey is the author of some thirteen books about the cuisine of her native India; therefore, it is not __________ that Indian food was important in her motion picture Chutney Popcorn.Offscreen, actor Madhur Jaffrey is the author of some thirteen books about the cuisine of her native India; therefore, it is not __________ that Indian food was important in her motion picture Chutney Popcorn.
答案：A, E　happenstance; incidental
foreordain
foreordain: standard unit headword
#486 SE
Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.Williamson had a fierce commitment to achieving an accord, spending enormous amount of time trying to forge a consensus out of an often __________ assembly.
答案：B, C　fractious; restive
consensuscynical
consensus: standard unit headword | cynical: standard unit headword
